rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).

rules_evaluated.evidence: line 15: 'Let it name the root CLAUDE.md, curriculum/CLAUDE.md, and the Claude Basics rules before opening anything manually.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Platform claims must be current and accurate for trainers running live demos.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 31: 'Run the customer build command for `acme claude-basics`.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The phrase names the customer slug and training key but omits the actual shell command (`node scripts/build-workbook.js acme claude-basics`). For a trainer-run live demo spine, the full command should be stated so the trainer knows what to type. TODO: add the actual command string. Not blocking — the maintainer block explicitly marks this as a spine, not a reading; and the claude-basics CLAUDE.md § Delivery architecture confirms the trainer operates the build system.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Ask Claude Code what rules govern Claude Basics work in this repo. Let it name the root CLAUDE.md, curriculum/CLAUDE.md, and the Claude Basics rules before opening anything manually.
platform_claims_checked.note: Asks Claude Code to find and name the files — does not assert they auto-load. curriculum/trainings/claude-basics/CLAUDE.md exists and is readable on demand. No false auto-load claim.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Run the customer build command for `acme claude-basics`.
platform_claims_checked.note: Correct customer slug and training key. Actual command confirmed in scripts/README.md: `node scripts/build-workbook.js acme claude-basics`. Command name omitted in demo spine — acceptable for a draft trainer spine but worth completing before first delivery.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Ask Claude Code to find the module order, source module files, shared exercises, and build command.

todos: line 31: 'Run the customer build command for `acme claude-basics`' — supply the full shell command (`node scripts/build-workbook.js acme claude-basics`) so the trainer knows exactly what to type during the live demo. Low risk at draft quality-state; resolve before first delivery.
